revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.
1 revision imported: importing redirects I created with chatGPT

revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template should be placed in the [[WP:LAYOUT#References|References section]] of an article if the article incorporates text from a source that is not under copyright and there is no source specific template listed in [[:Category:Attribution templates]]. :{{Source-attribution|''source and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} The four parameters are: *{{code|1}} (unnamed) = ''{{code|source name and other identifying details}}'' — The name of the ''source'', along with any other details which are needed to identify the work (the argument (the text) can also be another template such as {{tlx|cite book}}) *{{code|no-icon}}={{code|y}} — remove the leading icon (alternative {{code|noicon}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc) *pl=y — changes the wording from "{{green|from a publication}}" to "{{green|from publications}}" (alternative {{code|plural}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). *{{code|inline}}={{code|y}} — change the wording of the prescript from "{{green|This article...}}" to "{{green|One or more of the preceding sentences...}}" (alternative {{code|sentence}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). It is intended for use in inline citations. ==TemplateData== { "params": { "1": { "label": "Source", "description": "The name of the source, along with any other details which are needed to identify the work", "type": "content", "suggested": true }, "inline": { "aliases": [ "sentence" ], "label": "Inline?", "description": "Set to y to change \"This article\" to \"One or more of the preceding sentences\"", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "no-icon": { "aliases": [ "noicon" ], "label": "No icon?", "description": "Set to y to remove the leading icon", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "pl": { "aliases": [ "plural" ], "label": "Plural?", "description": "Set to y to change \"from a publication\" to \"from publications\"", "type": "boolean" } }, "paramOrder": [ "1", "no-icon", "pl", "inline" ], "description": "Adds a notice that an article incorporates information from a public domain source" } == Examples == #Using {{para|no-icon|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}}, removes the leading icon {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}} #A text string with no parameters (and followed by a citation), such as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}, would display as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}} #The template with a citation as the unnamed parameter, e.g. {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }}, shows as {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }} #Using {{para|pl|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|pl=y|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|pl=yes|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}} #Using {{para|inline|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|inline=y|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|inline=yes|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}} #Using all four parameters simultaniously {{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}}{{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} In the references section, these appear as {{reflist-talk}} == Hidden category == This template includes a hidden category—[[:Category:Source attribution]]—which does not appear at the bottom of an article page but does contain any article page that contains this template. == See also == *{{tl|Free-content attribution}} — prescript for a source that meets the definition of a [[Definition of Free Cultural Works|free cultural work]] * [[Wikipedia:Template_index/Sources_of_articles#Other]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Attribution templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Wikipedia article copyright templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: add {{wikidata link|Q...}} or [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template should be placed in the [[WP:LAYOUT#References|References section]] of an article if the article incorporates text from a source that is not under copyright and there is no source specific template listed in [[:Category:Attribution templates]]. :{{Source-attribution|''source and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} The four parameters are: *{{code|1}} (unnamed) = ''{{code|source name and other identifying details}}'' — The name of the ''source'', along with any other details which are needed to identify the work (the argument (the text) can also be another template such as {{tlx|cite book}}) *{{code|no-icon}}={{code|y}} — remove the leading icon (alternative {{code|noicon}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc) *pl=y — changes the wording from "{{green|from a publication}}" to "{{green|from publications}}" (alternative {{code|plural}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). *{{code|inline}}={{code|y}} — change the wording of the prescript from "{{green|This article...}}" to "{{green|One or more of the preceding sentences...}}" (alternative {{code|sentence}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). It is intended for use in inline citations. ==TemplateData== { "params": { "1": { "label": "Source", "description": "The name of the source, along with any other details which are needed to identify the work", "type": "content", "suggested": true }, "inline": { "aliases": [ "sentence" ], "label": "Inline?", "description": "Set to y to change \"This article\" to \"One or more of the preceding sentences\"", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "no-icon": { "aliases": [ "noicon" ], "label": "No icon?", "description": "Set to y to remove the leading icon", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "pl": { "aliases": [ "plural" ], "label": "Plural?", "description": "Set to y to change \"from a publication\" to \"from publications\"", "type": "boolean" } }, "paramOrder": [ "1", "no-icon", "pl", "inline" ], "description": "Adds a notice that an article incorporates information from a public domain source" } == Examples == #Using {{para|no-icon|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}}, removes the leading icon {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}} #A text string with no parameters (and followed by a citation), such as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}, would display as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}} #The template with a citation as the unnamed parameter, e.g. {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }}, shows as {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }} #Using {{para|pl|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|pl=y|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|pl=yes|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}} #Using {{para|inline|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|inline=y|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|inline=yes|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}} #Using all four parameters simultaniously {{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}}{{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} In the references section, these appear as {{reflist-talk}} == Hidden category == This template includes a hidden category—[[:Category:Source attribution]]—which does not appear at the bottom of an article page but does contain any article page that contains this template. == See also == *{{tl|Free-content attribution}} — prescript for a source that meets the definition of a [[Definition of Free Cultural Works|free cultural work]] * [[Wikipedia:Template_index/Sources_of_articles#Other]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Attribution templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Wikipedia article copyright templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] }} [[Category:Templates without wikidata]]

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Text replacement - "↵<noinclude>[[Category:Templates without wikidata]]</noinclude>" to ""
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template should be placed in the [[WP:LAYOUT#References|References section]] of an article if the article incorporates text from a source that is not under copyright and there is no source specific template listed in [[:Category:Attribution templates]]. :{{Source-attribution|''source and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} The four parameters are: *{{code|1}} (unnamed) = ''{{code|source name and other identifying details}}'' — The name of the ''source'', along with any other details which are needed to identify the work (the argument (the text) can also be another template such as {{tlx|cite book}}) *{{code|no-icon}}={{code|y}} — remove the leading icon (alternative {{code|noicon}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc) *pl=y — changes the wording from "{{green|from a publication}}" to "{{green|from publications}}" (alternative {{code|plural}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). *{{code|inline}}={{code|y}} — change the wording of the prescript from "{{green|This article...}}" to "{{green|One or more of the preceding sentences...}}" (alternative {{code|sentence}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). It is intended for use in inline citations. ==TemplateData== { "params": { "1": { "label": "Source", "description": "The name of the source, along with any other details which are needed to identify the work", "type": "content", "suggested": true }, "inline": { "aliases": [ "sentence" ], "label": "Inline?", "description": "Set to y to change \"This article\" to \"One or more of the preceding sentences\"", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "no-icon": { "aliases": [ "noicon" ], "label": "No icon?", "description": "Set to y to remove the leading icon", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "pl": { "aliases": [ "plural" ], "label": "Plural?", "description": "Set to y to change \"from a publication\" to \"from publications\"", "type": "boolean" } }, "paramOrder": [ "1", "no-icon", "pl", "inline" ], "description": "Adds a notice that an article incorporates information from a public domain source" } == Examples == #Using {{para|no-icon|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}}, removes the leading icon {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}} #A text string with no parameters (and followed by a citation), such as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}, would display as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}} #The template with a citation as the unnamed parameter, e.g. {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }}, shows as {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }} #Using {{para|pl|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|pl=y|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|pl=yes|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}} #Using {{para|inline|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|inline=y|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|inline=yes|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}} #Using all four parameters simultaniously {{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}}{{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} In the references section, these appear as {{reflist-talk}} == Hidden category == This template includes a hidden category—[[:Category:Source attribution]]—which does not appear at the bottom of an article page but does contain any article page that contains this template. == See also == *{{tl|Free-content attribution}} — prescript for a source that meets the definition of a [[Definition of Free Cultural Works|free cultural work]] * [[Wikipedia:Template_index/Sources_of_articles#Other]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Attribution templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Wikipedia article copyright templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] }}

revision.comment: Bot: Adding dummy category to make categories work after wiki restoration
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template should be placed in the [[WP:LAYOUT#References|References section]] of an article if the article incorporates text from a source that is not under copyright and there is no source specific template listed in [[:Category:Attribution templates]]. :{{Source-attribution|''source and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} The four parameters are: *{{code|1}} (unnamed) = ''{{code|source name and other identifying details}}'' — The name of the ''source'', along with any other details which are needed to identify the work (the argument (the text) can also be another template such as {{tlx|cite book}}) *{{code|no-icon}}={{code|y}} — remove the leading icon (alternative {{code|noicon}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc) *pl=y — changes the wording from "{{green|from a publication}}" to "{{green|from publications}}" (alternative {{code|plural}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). *{{code|inline}}={{code|y}} — change the wording of the prescript from "{{green|This article...}}" to "{{green|One or more of the preceding sentences...}}" (alternative {{code|sentence}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). It is intended for use in inline citations. ==TemplateData== { "params": { "1": { "label": "Source", "description": "The name of the source, along with any other details which are needed to identify the work", "type": "content", "suggested": true }, "inline": { "aliases": [ "sentence" ], "label": "Inline?", "description": "Set to y to change \"This article\" to \"One or more of the preceding sentences\"", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "no-icon": { "aliases": [ "noicon" ], "label": "No icon?", "description": "Set to y to remove the leading icon", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "pl": { "aliases": [ "plural" ], "label": "Plural?", "description": "Set to y to change \"from a publication\" to \"from publications\"", "type": "boolean" } }, "paramOrder": [ "1", "no-icon", "pl", "inline" ], "description": "Adds a notice that an article incorporates information from a public domain source" } == Examples == #Using {{para|no-icon|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}}, removes the leading icon {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}} #A text string with no parameters (and followed by a citation), such as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}, would display as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}} #The template with a citation as the unnamed parameter, e.g. {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }}, shows as {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }} #Using {{para|pl|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|pl=y|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|pl=yes|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}} #Using {{para|inline|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|inline=y|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|inline=yes|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}} #Using all four parameters simultaniously {{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}}{{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} In the references section, these appear as {{reflist-talk}} == Hidden category == This template includes a hidden category—[[:Category:Source attribution]]—which does not appear at the bottom of an article page but does contain any article page that contains this template. == See also == *{{tl|Free-content attribution}} — prescript for a source that meets the definition of a [[Definition of Free Cultural Works|free cultural work]] * [[Wikipedia:Template_index/Sources_of_articles#Other]] {{Sandbox other|| [[Category:Attribution templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Wikipedia article copyright templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] }} [[Category:Templates]]

revision.comment: Bot: wrap 3 stray tag(s) in noinclude [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23606305292|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template should be placed in the [[WP:LAYOUT#References|References section]] of an article if the article incorporates text from a source that is not under copyright and there is no source specific template listed in [[:Category:Attribution templates]]. :{{Source-attribution|''source and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} The four parameters are: *{{code|1}} (unnamed) = ''{{code|source name and other identifying details}}'' — The name of the ''source'', along with any other details which are needed to identify the work (the argument (the text) can also be another template such as {{tlx|cite book}}) *{{code|no-icon}}={{code|y}} — remove the leading icon (alternative {{code|noicon}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc) *pl=y — changes the wording from "{{green|from a publication}}" to "{{green|from publications}}" (alternative {{code|plural}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). *{{code|inline}}={{code|y}} — change the wording of the prescript from "{{green|This article...}}" to "{{green|One or more of the preceding sentences...}}" (alternative {{code|sentence}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). It is intended for use in inline citations. ==TemplateData== { "params": { "1": { "label": "Source", "description": "The name of the source, along with any other details which are needed to identify the work", "type": "content", "suggested": true }, "inline": { "aliases": [ "sentence" ], "label": "Inline?", "description": "Set to y to change \"This article\" to \"One or more of the preceding sentences\"", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "no-icon": { "aliases": [ "noicon" ], "label": "No icon?", "description": "Set to y to remove the leading icon", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "pl": { "aliases": [ "plural" ], "label": "Plural?", "description": "Set to y to change \"from a publication\" to \"from publications\"", "type": "boolean" } }, "paramOrder": [ "1", "no-icon", "pl", "inline" ], "description": "Adds a notice that an article incorporates information from a public domain source" } == Examples == #Using {{para|no-icon|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}}, removes the leading icon {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}} #A text string with no parameters (and followed by a citation), such as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}, would display as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}} #The template with a citation as the unnamed parameter, e.g. {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }}, shows as {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }} #Using {{para|pl|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|pl=y|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|pl=yes|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}} #Using {{para|inline|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|inline=y|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|inline=yes|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}} #Using all four parameters simultaniously {{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}}{{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} In the references section, these appear as {{reflist-talk}} == Hidden category == This template includes a hidden category—[[:Category:Source attribution]]—which does not appear at the bottom of an article page but does contain any article page that contains this template. == See also == *{{tl|Free-content attribution}} — prescript for a source that meets the definition of a [[Definition of Free Cultural Works|free cultural work]] * [[Wikipedia:Template_index/Sources_of_articles#Other]] {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Attribution templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Wikipedia article copyright templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]

revision.comment: Bot: strip HTML comments [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24975162458|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text: {{Documentation subpage}} {{High-use}} == Usage == This template should be placed in the [[WP:LAYOUT#References|References section]] of an article if the article incorporates text from a source that is not under copyright and there is no source specific template listed in [[:Category:Attribution templates]]. :{{Source-attribution|''source and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} The four parameters are: *{{code|1}} (unnamed) = ''{{code|source name and other identifying details}}'' — The name of the ''source'', along with any other details which are needed to identify the work (the argument (the text) can also be another template such as {{tlx|cite book}}) *{{code|no-icon}}={{code|y}} — remove the leading icon (alternative {{code|noicon}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc) *pl=y — changes the wording from "{{green|from a publication}}" to "{{green|from publications}}" (alternative {{code|plural}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). *{{code|inline}}={{code|y}} — change the wording of the prescript from "{{green|This article...}}" to "{{green|One or more of the preceding sentences...}}" (alternative {{code|sentence}} and the argument can be any string such as {{green|''yes''}} or {{green|''1''}} etc). It is intended for use in inline citations. ==TemplateData== { "params": { "1": { "label": "Source", "description": "The name of the source, along with any other details which are needed to identify the work", "type": "content", "suggested": true }, "inline": { "aliases": [ "sentence" ], "label": "Inline?", "description": "Set to y to change \"This article\" to \"One or more of the preceding sentences\"", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "no-icon": { "aliases": [ "noicon" ], "label": "No icon?", "description": "Set to y to remove the leading icon", "type": "boolean", "suggested": false }, "pl": { "aliases": [ "plural" ], "label": "Plural?", "description": "Set to y to change \"from a publication\" to \"from publications\"", "type": "boolean" } }, "paramOrder": [ "1", "no-icon", "pl", "inline" ], "description": "Adds a notice that an article incorporates information from a public domain source" } == Examples == #Using {{para|no-icon|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}}, removes the leading icon {{Source-attribution|no-icon=y}} #A text string with no parameters (and followed by a citation), such as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}, would display as {{Source attribution}} {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}} #The template with a citation as the unnamed parameter, e.g. {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }}, shows as {{Source-attribution|{{cite book |title= Handbook to the cathedrals of England |first=Richard |last=John |date=1869}} }} #Using {{para|pl|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|pl=y|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|pl=yes|John, Richard (1869). ''Handbook to the cathedrals of England''; and {{cite book|last=Offa |first=Anne |year=1066|title=An Old Book}}}} #Using {{para|inline|y}}, as in {{Source-attribution|inline=y|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}}, shows as {{Source-attribution|inline=yes|''Handbook to the cathedrals of England'', by Richard John (1869)}} #Using all four parameters simultaniously {{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}}{{Source-attribution|''sources and other details''|no-icon=y |pl=y |inline=y}} In the references section, these appear as {{reflist-talk}} == Hidden category == This template includes a hidden category—[[:Category:Source attribution]]—which does not appear at the bottom of an article page but does contain any article page that contains this template. == See also == *{{tl|Free-content attribution}} — prescript for a source that meets the definition of a [[Definition of Free Cultural Works|free cultural work]] * [[Wikipedia:Template_index/Sources_of_articles#Other]] {{Sandbox other|| }} [[Category:Attribution templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Wikipedia article copyright templates|*{{PAGENAME}}]] [[Category:Templates]] [[Category:Templates fixed with noinclude]]
